steps.title: Set up the absolute value equation and prepare to backsolve
steps.content:
We need to find $k$ such that $f(k) = 3k$, which gives us $$|59 - 2k| = 3k$$
Since the answer choices are simple fractions, we can test them directly rather than solve algebraically.

steps.title: Test choices B and C
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B ($k = \frac{59}{2}$):
$$|59 - 2 \cdot \frac{59}{2}| = |59 - 59| = 0$$
$$3k = 3 \cdot \frac{59}{2} = \frac{177}{2}$$
Since $0 \neq \frac{177}{2}$, this choice fails.

Fails. Testing choice C ($k = \frac{177}{5}$):
$$|59 - 2 \cdot \frac{177}{5}| = |59 - \frac{354}{5}| = |\frac{295 - 354}{5}| = |\frac{-59}{5}| = \frac{59}{5}$$
$$3k = 3 \cdot \frac{177}{5} = \frac{531}{5}$$
Since $\frac{59}{5} \neq \frac{531}{5}$, this choice also fails.

steps.title: Test choice A
steps.content:
Testing choice A ($k = \frac{59}{5}$):
$$|59 - 2 \cdot \frac{59}{5}| = |59 - \frac{118}{5}| = |\frac{295 - 118}{5}| = \frac{177}{5}$$
$$3k = 3 \cdot \frac{59}{5} = \frac{177}{5}$$
Both sides equal $\frac{177}{5}$, so the equation is satisfied. That matches choice A — A is correct.
